title: solanum medians f. neoweberbaueri
intro: Solanum medians f. neoweberbaueri, also known as the New Pau Pereira, is a plant species within the Solanaceae family. It is a climbing vine native to Brazil, used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including relieving coughs and sore throats. New Pau Pereira is traditionally prepared as a herbal tea or used in infusions for its medicinal benefits, although further research is needed to fully confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
